summary: The diff implements REQ-1 (modified getMediaAssets), REQ-2 (retained deleteMediaAsset admin checks), and REQ-4 (frontend media loading improvements). However, REQ-3 verification evidence is missing as upload logic is not shown in the diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Verify media upload logic remains unmodified and continues to function correctly
missing_requirements.reason: The diff does not include changes to uploadMediaAsset function or any media upload logic files. While the constraint states not to modify upload logic, there is no evidence in the diff that this was verified or tested. MediaUploadZone.tsx is not shown in the modified files.

hallucinated_features.description: Enhanced error classification logic in useMediaAssets.ts with multiple error type detection functions (isAuthError, isNetworkError, isCanisterStoppedError)
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest does not mention adding error handling enhancements or error classification logic. The request focuses on removing principal validation from getMediaAssets and verifying existing functionality, not adding new error detection mechanisms.

hallucinated_features.description: Blob URL management with cleanup and revocation in MediaAssetGrid component using useEffect
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest does not request blob URL lifecycle management or memory cleanup features. The request only mentions verifying that media loads correctly, not implementing new blob URL handling logic.

hallucinated_features.description: Delegation identity validation utility with validateDelegationIdentity function calls
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest does not mention adding delegation identity validation utilities. The request focuses on removing strict principal validation from getMediaAssets, not adding new validation mechanisms elsewhere.

hallucinated_features.description: Enhanced console logging throughout media asset operations
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest does not request adding extensive debug logging. While logging may help with testing (REQ-4), the comprehensive logging added appears to go beyond the stated requirements.
